source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

text: Let the eyes and face be soft, loosen the jaw.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

text: Relax, the shoulders and let your arms and hands rest easily.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch

text: Let the belly be soft and the breath natural.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And let the heart be soft as well to receive whatever arises with a spirit of kindness, compassion.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And now with this embodied presence, begin to pay attention to the miraculous play of experience that arises the thoughts and sounds and feelings and sensations.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Let yourself be present just where you are for the life that moves through you with mindfulness or let's call it loving awareness.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And notice what displays itself as you sit in the present.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And after just a minute or so, you may notice that the attention wanders from the present planning, remembering imagining, problem-solving, judging, liking, disliking, all different things that take you away from being just here where you are.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So to help deepen the sense of presence, underneath the waves of thoughts and plans and sensations and sounds, become aware of the wave of your breath, how your body is breathing itself.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And feel it is coolness in the nostrils, the upper lip, swirling tingling in the back of the throat, or the rise in fall of chest or belly.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And if it's at all difficult to sense the breath, put one hand on your belly and leave it there for this meditation time, and feel the rise and fall of your belly in the palm of your hand.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And feeling each breath in bite a sense of calm or ease to grow, bringing mind and body together with each breath as you breathe in and breathe out.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And again, after just a minute or two, you notice that frequently the attention moves off of the breath.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This breath, this breath, ease and calm, and guided, as it comes in, ease and calm and guided, as you breathe out.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One more instruction that can be helpful, as you've been sensing the breath, the other sounds, thoughts and sensations, horizon fall around the breath like waves of the ocean, with the breath in the center of the moll.

source: Jack Kornfield Guided Meditation_ Coming Back Home To Yourself__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
